title: Round 2 Ambiguity Dispositions for RFC-0001 Through RFC-0003
closure_rule: Internal acceptance does not close an ambiguity while required external, affected-person, legal, or profile review remains incomplete.

items.finding: The materiality criteria are explicit, conservative under uncertainty, and operationally testable.
items.next_gate: Required external, affected-person, legal, or domain review before closure

items.finding: High-impact use now has explicit consequence classes and required accountability fields.
items.next_gate: Required external, affected-person, legal, or domain review before closure

items.finding: The RFC requires a participation profile but does not yet define reference age, capacity, jurisdiction, conflict, and service profiles.
items.next_gate: Residual source/profile revision and Round 3 review

items.finding: The governed disclosure basis defines purpose, authority, minimum necessity, recipient, limits, notice, review, and appeal.
items.next_gate: Required external, affected-person, legal, or domain review before closure

items.finding: Technical processing categories are separated from jurisdiction-specific legal qualification.
items.next_gate: Required external, affected-person, legal, or domain review before closure

items.finding: Authority fields are defined, but proportionate evidence standards and representative types still require a profile.
items.next_gate: Residual source/profile revision and Round 3 review

items.finding: Retention profile structure is clear, but reference durations, backup completion, and review triggers remain unspecified.
items.next_gate: Residual source/profile revision and Round 3 review

items.finding: The authority map and restrictive conflict precedence resolve the architectural authority question.
items.next_gate: Required external, affected-person, legal, or domain review before closure

items.finding: High-impact safety action, P0/P1, actual authority, handoff states, and closure authority are explicit.
items.next_gate: Required external, affected-person, legal, or domain review before closure

items.finding: The proportionality record now covers competing harms, uncertainty, alternatives, privacy cost, duration, rollback, and review.
items.next_gate: Required external, affected-person, legal, or domain review before closure

items.finding: Service profiles are explicit, but credible response targets and independent resource-freshness verification still require operational profiles.
items.next_gate: Residual source/profile revision and Round 3 review

items.finding: Conflict triggers, interim restrictions, independent authority, minimum disclosure, later notice, and appeal are explicit.
items.next_gate: Required external, affected-person, legal, or domain review before closure
